title: Condemning the Members of Congress constituting the leadership team of the House Republican Conference.
plain_summary: This congressional resolution criticizes the Republican leaders in the House of Representatives. It says they allowed their political group to post a bad message that questioned whether Congressman Adriano Espaillat is a real American citizen and patriot. The resolution claims this anti-immigrant language breaks the promises that members of Congress make when they take office. The resolution says this kind of harmful language should not be used in Congress.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ethics.

sections.title: Resolution Condemning the Members of Congress constituting the leadership team of the House Republican Conference
sections.text: This resolution condemns the House Republican Conference leadership team. On March fifth, two thousand twenty five, the House Republican Conference posted a harmful message on X (formerly Twitter) about United States Congressman Adriano Espaillat of New York. He is the Chairman of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus (a group of Hispanic members of Congress). The post questioned his immigration status and his loyalty to America. This type of language that targets people based on their background shows that even when someone follows the law, gets legal status, becomes a United States citizen, gets elected to Congress, and takes multiple oaths to defend the Constitution, they can still be wrongly called an illegal immigrant. This anti-immigrant language goes against the oath that members of Congress take when they enter office. It does not belong in the House of Representatives. This post came after an intense debate on the House floor about proper behavior and the need for Congress members to rise above political fighting and improve how they talk to each other in this important place of American democracy. Therefore, this resolution condemns Speaker Mike Johnson, Majority Leader Steve Scalise, Majority Whip Tom Emmer, House Republican Chair Lisa McClain, Republican Policy Committee Chairman Kevin Hern, House Republican Conference Vice Chair Blake Moore, Deputy Whip Guy Reschenthaler, and National Republican Congressional Committee Chairman Richard Hudson. They are being condemned for allowing their political organization to make a false statement and question the citizenship of their fellow Congress member, Congressman Adriano Espaillat.
